description: A cropped screenshot of a news article header rather than an agency-designed graphic. On a plain white background, a small red rounded tag reads 'DEPORTATION' at top center. Below it is a large bold black sans-serif headline: 'ICE says it deported 174 criminal migrants from Texas, including a man with 39 illegal entries,' followed by a gray sub-headline: 'ICE says the 174 accounted for 610 criminal convictions, including 4 convictions for rape or sexual assault.' There is no photograph, logo, or agency seal visible in the crop; it is purely the text block of an online news story shared to amplify enforcement statistics. The framing emphasizes large arrest numbers and severe crimes to support the 'Make America Safe Again' deportation messaging. Minimal custom DHS design, so marked not a designed meme. Target audience: general public and supporters.
notable_text: DEPORTATION; ICE says it deported 174 criminal migrants from Texas, including a man with 39 illegal entries; ICE says the 174 accounted for 610 criminal convictions, including 4 convictions for rape or sexual assault
